tweets.name: Marc Andreessen
tweets.text: Thesis: the problem with AI working in every domain = all the edge cases. Antithesis: domains with lots of edge cases = difficult & time consuming to practically impossible for error-prone people. Synthesis: such domains = where AI agents will do best. (Such as SAAS migration…)

tweets.name: Jason Calacanis
tweets.text:
Here’s the truth: we’ve already reached AGI — we just haven’t implemented it broadly. 

Millions of jobs are being lost as we speak. 

Entire careers will be retired. 

The rich and powerful investors and founders who implement AGI will get bizarrely rich beyond what makes sense.

tweets.name: Michael Seibel
tweets.text: Too many investors focus on the "successes" of their Series B companies when advising early stage companies.  They don't understand how many of their Series B companies will die.  Too few investors have spent time with long term successful businesses.  Founders take note.

tweets.name: Garry Tan
tweets.text:
Want to make new software the way I do? I only started 72 days ago. 

I am now on pace to do 90X the amount of software engineering than I did the last time I was working hard on code in 2013. 

Literally with AI it's like I have 90 versions of myself coding. https://t.co/rFqmo4eKYV

tweets.name: Garry Tan
tweets.text: And this is nights/weekends work too. I still have full full days at work at YC. https://t.co/CD7seiRDOu

Claire Vo's first day with @OpenClaw it deleted her family calendar.

Now she runs 9 agents across 3 Mac Minis, and said "I haven't felt like this since I was a teenager learning to code."

Her sales agent Sam does a daily CRM sweep, identifies decision-makers from new signups,

tweets.name: Venture Twins
tweets.text:
I know we all complain about the algorithm sometimes - but you can't deny X has improved a TON. 

Auto-translation is one example. Search is also way better, and tagging Grok is genuinely useful (and sometimes very funny) now.

And Imagine is getting crazy good at image + video.

tweets.name: Venture Twins
tweets.text: Still not over the fact that we can rent intelligence now

tweets.name: Venture Twins
tweets.text:
My entire feed is sick Pretext demos.

It's truly insane to see how much we can do with coding agents - there's already an entire ecosystem around a project that shipped on Friday night.

Also what a case for open source software 👌 https://t.co/ZocRAtQ6GW

tweets.name: Venture Twins
tweets.text:
There’s a crazy cool new AI feature in Google Maps.

“Ask Maps” uses Gemini to answer location-based questions in natural language. 

It’s like a personal researcher that helps you find places and plan your day.

I’ve been using it to search by vibe (and find parking) 👇 https://t.co/OxpmMB620D
